paragraph1: Since 1988, LOG‑it has been the prestigious logbook for divers who value quality, durability, and heritage. Created by Kelvin Pearce, a passionate diver who believed every dive deserved to be remembered with pride.
paragraph2: For over three decades, LOG‑it has been trusted by divers across the UK, Ireland, Europe, the USA, New Zealand, and Antarctica. Each edition is printed in the UK by Henry Ling in Dorchester — a commitment to craftsmanship that remains uncompromising.

milestones.title: Kelvin Pearce Creates LOG‑it
milestones.description: A passionate diver from the 1980s created the first LOG‑it logbook to help divers record memories they could look back on for decades.

milestones.title: Janet & Justin Take Over
milestones.description: Kelvin passed LOG‑it to Janet and Justin, who modernised the book while keeping its original spirit. They sold across UK, Ireland, and internationally.

milestones.title: Dan & Eve Era Begins
milestones.description: On 30 September 2023, LOG‑it was taken over by Dan (Wheelsdan) and his wife Eve. As active divers and instructors, they remain committed to UK manufacturing and premium quality.

values.title: Long‑Lasting
values.description: Hard‑wearing craftsmanship built to last decades.

values.title: Community‑Driven
values.description: Divers can suggest improvements to the book.

values.title: UK Made
values.description: Printed in Dorchester by Henry Ling since day one.
